It is still a problem to find conditions, sufficient and necessary at the same time, guaranteeing uniqueness in law of weak solutions of (2) in dimensions greater than 2 (see e.g. abstract of [23]).
title: Uniqueness in law criteria for weak solutions of multidimensional Ito SDEs
background_latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P)\) be a filtered probability space supporting a standard \(d\)-dimensional Brownian motion \(W\). Consider the It\^o stochastic differential equation (SDE)
\[
 dX_t = b(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad X_0=x\in\mathbb R^d,
\]
where \(b:[0,T]\times\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R^d\) and \(\sigma:[0,T]\times\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R^{d\times d}\) are Borel measurable coefficients. A \emph{weak solution} is a tuple \((\Omega',\mathcal F',(\mathcal F'_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P',W',X')\) such that \(W'\) is a Brownian motion and \(X'\) is an adapted continuous process satisfying the SDE in the It\^o sense under \(\mathbb P'\). 

The SDE is said to have \emph{uniqueness in law} (also called \emph{weak uniqueness}) if for any two weak solutions with the same initial condition \(x\), the laws of the solution processes \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) on path space coincide. One often assumes some form of (uniform) nondegeneracy/ellipticity of \(\sigma\), e.g. \(\sigma\sigma^\top\ge \lambda I\) for some \(\lambda>0\), but coefficients may still be merely measurable or discontinuous.

In the attached paper, the finite-player game dynamics lead to an \(n\)-dimensional SDE of the form
\[
 dX_t = D(t,X_t)\,dW_t,
\]
with diagonal diffusion matrix \(D(t,x)\) whose diagonal entries are bounded between two positive constants \(\sigma_1<\sigma_2\) (uniform ellipticity), but can be discontinuous as functions of \(x\) because they arise from feedback controls. The paper notes that, in dimensions greater than 2, general necessary-and-sufficient conditions for uniqueness in law of such weak solutions are not known.
problem_definition_latex:
For dimensions \(d>2\), characterize (with conditions that are simultaneously sufficient and necessary) when the It\^o SDE
\[
 dX_t = b(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad X_0=x\in\mathbb R^d,
\]
admits uniqueness in law of weak solutions.

In particular, determine such a sharp characterization for the driftless, uniformly elliptic case
\[
 dX_t = \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,
\]
including the case where \(\sigma(t,\cdot)\) may be discontinuous (e.g. piecewise constant) and may be restricted to be diagonal-valued.
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P)\) be a filtered probability space supporting a standard \(d\)-dimensional Brownian motion \(W\). Consider the It\^o stochastic differential equation (SDE)
\[
 dX_t = b(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad X_0=x\in\mathbb R^d,
\]
where \(b:[0,T]\times\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R^d\) and \(\sigma:[0,T]\times\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R^{d\times d}\) are Borel measurable coefficients. A \emph{weak solution} is a tuple \((\Omega',\mathcal F',(\mathcal F'_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P',W',X')\) such that \(W'\) is a Brownian motion and \(X'\) is an adapted continuous process satisfying the SDE in the It\^o sense under \(\mathbb P'\). 

The SDE is said to have \emph{uniqueness in law} (also called \emph{weak uniqueness}) if for any two weak solutions with the same initial condition \(x\), the laws of the solution processes \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) on path space coincide. One often assumes some form of (uniform) nondegeneracy/ellipticity of \(\sigma\), e.g. \(\sigma\sigma^\top\ge \lambda I\) for some \(\lambda>0\), but coefficients may still be merely measurable or discontinuous.

In the attached paper, the finite-player game dynamics lead to an \(n\)-dimensional SDE of the form
\[
 dX_t = D(t,X_t)\,dW_t,
\]
with diagonal diffusion matrix \(D(t,x)\) whose diagonal entries are bounded between two positive constants \(\sigma_1<\sigma_2\) (uniform ellipticity), but can be discontinuous as functions of \(x\) because they arise from feedback controls. The paper notes that, in dimensions greater than 2, general necessary-and-sufficient conditions for uniqueness in law of such weak solutions are not known.

For dimensions \(d>2\), characterize (with conditions that are simultaneously sufficient and necessary) when the It\^o SDE
\[
 dX_t = b(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad X_0=x\in\mathbb R^d,
\]
admits uniqueness in law of weak solutions.

In particular, determine such a sharp characterization for the driftless, uniformly elliptic case
\[
 dX_t = \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,
\]
including the case where \(\sigma(t,\cdot)\) may be discontinuous (e.g. piecewise constant) and may be restricted to be diagonal-valued.
open_problem_quote: It is still a problem to find conditions, sufficient and necessary at the same time, guaranteeing uniqueness in law of weak solutions of (2) in dimensions greater than 2 (see e.g. abstract of [23]).
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for a sharp necessary-and-sufficient characterization across a broad class of multidimensional SDEs, not a single conjectured inequality or finite-dimensional extremal instance. Automated search might find examples of non-uniqueness/uniqueness for specific coefficient families, but there is no clear finite search space or decisive empirical test that would resolve the full characterization.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary loop could explore parametrized families of discontinuous or piecewise-constant diffusion coefficients \(\sigma\) and numerically probe for non-uniqueness indicators (e.g., by constructing distinct weak solutions via different approximation schemes) to suggest conjectures or counterexamples. However, uniqueness in law is not straightforward to certify computationally, and numerical evidence may be unreliable without rigorous analysis, limiting suitability to medium.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper’s game-theoretic motivation leads to driftless, uniformly elliptic SDEs with discontinuous (often piecewise constant) diffusion coefficients, notably diagonal matrices arising from feedback diffusion controls. Existence of weak solutions in this regime is classical (e.g. via Krylov’s uniformly elliptic theory and the Stroock–Varadhan martingale problem framework), but uniqueness in law in dimensions \(d>2\) is not governed by a single sharp criterion comparable to the one-dimensional bounded-variation theory (Nakao) or the two-dimensional measurable-coefficient results (Krylov 1969) used in the paper’s \(n=2\) analysis.

Known positive results toward weak uniqueness in higher dimensions typically impose additional regularity or structure: Hölder (or better) continuity of the diffusion matrix yields well-posed martingale problems (Bass–Chen), while singular drifts can be handled under integrability assumptions when the diffusion is sufficiently regular/nondegenerate (Zvonkin; Veretennikov; Krylov–Röckner). These results, however, are not necessary-and-sufficient, and they do not cover the discontinuous, state-dependent, uniformly elliptic diffusion matrices (including diagonal, piecewise constant ones) that naturally appear in diffusion-control games.

Accordingly, the open problem remains: to characterize, in \(d>2\), when the martingale problem for \(\tfrac12\mathrm{tr}(a\nabla^2)+b\cdot\nabla\) with merely measurable coefficients is well-posed, and in particular to find sharp criteria for driftless uniformly elliptic SDEs with discontinuous \(\sigma\). Promising directions suggested by the existing literature include identifying geometric/analytic conditions on discontinuity interfaces (e.g. transmission conditions for divergence/nondivergence form operators), developing invariance/Dirichlet-form criteria for uniqueness of associated Markov processes, and isolating special classes (such as diagonal diffusions with coordinatewise discontinuities) where PDE methods or coupling arguments might yield a complete characterization.

sources.title: On Itô’s stochastic integral equations
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes foundational existence/uniqueness-in-law results for certain multidimensional Itô SDEs with measurable coefficients under structural conditions; in particular provides a two-dimensional weak uniqueness theorem used in the source paper for the 2-player case.
sources.connection: Serves as a classical positive result on weak uniqueness for measurable coefficients in low dimension, highlighting the dimension-dependence that motivates the open problem for \(d>2\).

sources.title: Controlled Diffusion Processes
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides existence of weak solutions for uniformly elliptic SDEs with merely measurable coefficients (via martingale problem/PDE methods), a key tool for driftless uniformly elliptic dynamics with discontinuous diffusion.
sources.connection: Gives the baseline existence theory used in the source paper for the diagonal, uniformly elliptic diffusion matrix \(D(t,x)\); does not yield a sharp necessary-and-sufficient weak uniqueness characterization in \(d>2\).

sources.title: Multidimensional Diffusion Processes
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops the martingale problem framework and links weak uniqueness to uniqueness for the associated martingale problem/generator; provides broad sufficient conditions and counterexamples for uniqueness in law.
sources.connection: Frames the open problem as seeking necessary-and-sufficient conditions for uniqueness of the martingale problem for generators \(\tfrac12\mathrm{tr}(a\nabla^2)+b\cdot\nabla\) with measurable coefficients in \(d>2\), including uniformly elliptic but discontinuous \(a\).

sources.title: On strong solutions and explicit formulas for solutions of stochastic integral equations
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows strong existence and uniqueness (hence weak uniqueness) for SDEs with bounded measurable drift and nondegenerate constant diffusion (and extensions), via Zvonkin-type transforms.
sources.connection: Illustrates that for uniformly elliptic diffusions, adding regularity/structure (e.g. constant \(\sigma\)) yields uniqueness in law even with merely measurable drift; however does not cover discontinuous state-dependent \(\sigma\) in \(d>2\).

sources.title: A transformation of the phase space of a diffusion process that removes the drift
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces the Zvonkin transform removing irregular drift under nondegeneracy, leading to (strong/weak) uniqueness results for SDEs with singular drift.
sources.connection: Relevant as a key technique for weak uniqueness when \(b\) is merely measurable but \(\sigma\) is sufficiently regular/nondegenerate; does not address the driftless but discontinuous \(\sigma\) regime emphasized by the source paper.

sources.title: Stochastic differential equations with Hölder continuous coefficients and supermartingale problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides weak uniqueness/well-posedness for martingale problems under Hölder continuity of the diffusion matrix (and related conditions), using analytic/probabilistic techniques.
sources.connection: Gives sharp-ish sufficient conditions beyond Lipschitz for weak uniqueness in \(d\ge 2\), but still far from necessary-and-sufficient and does not cover discontinuous piecewise-constant \(\sigma\) in \(d>2\).

sources.title: Strong solutions of stochastic equations with singular time dependent drift
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes strong well-posedness (hence weak uniqueness) for SDEs with drift in \(L^q_t L^p_x\) under nondegeneracy and regularity of diffusion (often constant or sufficiently smooth).
sources.connection: Shows that for \(d>2\) one can obtain uniqueness in law under integrability conditions on \(b\) when \(\sigma\) is regular; complements the source paper’s focus on discontinuous \(\sigma\) arising from feedback diffusion control.

sources.title: On the pathwise uniqueness of solutions of one-dimensional stochastic differential equations
sources.theorem_or_result: Proves pathwise uniqueness for certain 1D SDEs with diffusion coefficients of bounded variation (including discontinuous piecewise constant), implying strong existence/uniqueness in 1D.
sources.connection: Explains why the oscillating Brownian motion (1D, piecewise constant \(\sigma\)) used in the source paper is strongly well-posed; highlights the stark contrast with the multidimensional \(d>2\) case where such discontinuities can break weak uniqueness.
